**Scenario B — Activate overtime.** 6 OT agents across both teams for the afternoon shift. Both teams above 78%. Cost: $4,200 today, pushes Q2 OT to 74% of budget.

**Scenario C — Skills-based overflow to digital banking.** Route low-complexity auto loan calls to digital banking (currently at 91%). Auto loans projects to 79%, general banking holds at 72%, digital banking drops to 84% but stays healthy. Net aggregate: **79%**. Lowest cost, highest aggregate recovery.

Scenario C is the recommendation.

nfcu_dir_cascade_impact.ai_message:
Here is the cascade. The auto loan promo at 7:15 AM drove a 340% queue spike. Priya pulled 8 cross-trained agents from general banking. That move cleared auto loans wait time to manageable, **but it also dropped general banking from 87% to 72% service level**. General banking is now your second-most-exposed queue — a queue that was healthy 90 minutes ago. Mortgage servicing is independent of this cascade — it's running at 64% because of the 3 agents on extended leave, a structural staffing gap.

This is exactly the kind of cross-team correlation Dynamics 365 alone cannot show you. Each manager sees their own queue. Only the AI roll-up sees that Priya's intervention triggered Okafor's degradation.

nfcu_dir_priority_call.ai_message:
**Prioritize general banking.** Three reasons:

**(1) Cascade containment.** Auto loans is already in active intervention with a 30-minute recovery path. General banking is in a *worsening* state with no intervention applied yet. Containing the cascade is more valuable than further accelerating an already-recovering queue.

**(2) Member impact.** General banking handles 38% of weekly call volume — the highest of any queue. A 1-point drop in general banking SL affects more members than a 1-point drop in auto loans.

**(3) Cost.** Reversing the cascade with Scenario A is $0. Doubling down on auto loans (Scenario B) costs $4,200 today and pushes Q2 OT toward the projected 138% trajectory.

Mortgage servicing is the deeper structural risk, but the 3 agents return in late May — staffing math improves on its own.
suggested_chips: What are my options to stabilize both teams?

confidence.rationale: Decision logic is strong; volume-distribution and cost numbers are authoritative. Trade-offs across the three reasons add modest variance.

nfcu_dir_migration_detail.ai_message:
Here are the 3 IVR flows currently failing parity testing:

**(1) Mortgage Payment Processing** — Genesys uses URS conditional routing with 4 skill requirements (mortgage, payments, Spanish optional, after-hours fallback). Dynamics 365 implementation routes correctly on 3 of 4 conditions but drops the after-hours fallback when Spanish is selected. Volume: 14% of total calls.

**(2) Fraud Alert Escalation** — Genesys auto-escalates to a tier-2 fraud queue with priority routing. Dynamics 365 implementation correctly escalates but loses the priority flag, so fraud alerts queue behind standard inquiries. Volume: 9% of total calls.

**(3) Spanish-Language Routing** — Genesys has 8 skill-tagged Spanish agents with specialty sub-skills (mortgage, auto, deposits). Dynamics 365 currently routes to a single Spanish queue without sub-skill matching, increasing transfer rate and handle time. Volume: 8% of total calls.

Combined volume at risk: **31%** of all calls. The SI partner has the configuration export from Genesys and the Dynamics 365 destination rules — they have the artifacts to fix this.
suggested_chips: Draft an escalation to the SI partner

nfcu_dir_phase_2_options.ai_message:
Three options to accelerate Phase 2:

**Option 1 — Escalate to SI partner with hard deadline.** Send the drafted memo today demanding a 48-hour remediation plan. Historical pattern shows your SI partner moves on documented escalations within 72 hours. Cost: $0. Risk: still depends on SI partner capacity.

**Option 2 — Bring in a secondary integrator.** Engage one of the 2 NFCU-approved backup integrators for parallel work on the 3 failing flows. Cost: ~$45K for 2 weeks of work. Cuts ~7 days from the slip. Requires VP approval.

**Option 3 — Phase 2 partial cutover.** Move the 11 working IVR flows to Dynamics 365 on schedule and keep the 3 failing flows on Genesys until parity passes. Cost: $0 incremental. Risk: violates 'one platform per phase' migration principle and complicates Phase 3.

My recommendation: **Option 1 today, Option 2 standby if no SI response by April 28.** Option 3 only if Option 1 and 2 both stall.

nfcu_dir_budget_cuts.ai_message:

**(1) Tighten cross-coverage authorization to manager-approval-only.** Currently any senior agent can approve cross-team coverage at OT rate. Restricting to manager approval projects $28K savings over remaining Q2 with minimal SL impact (historical data suggests <2pt aggregate drop).

Combined: $83K — closes most of the $98K residual gap. Each lever has different risk profiles and trade-offs; happy to go deeper on any of them.
